claims.claim: The average internal temperature of a cell during TR propagation was approximately 160°C higher than the average maximum temperature measured at the cell surface between cells, representing approximately a 17% increase.
claims.quote: The average temperature inside the cell was approximately 160 °C higher than the temperature at the cell surface between the cells (17% increase).

claims.claim: In modules with parallel electrical connections, the TR propagation time interval first increased up to the sixth cell and then decreased and stabilized, due to pre-heating of cells near the heater followed by overall thermal acceleration in later cells.
claims.quote: It is interesting to point out that the propagation time increased until the sixth cell, then decreased to a lower level and stayed stable. The pattern applies to all the connection scenarios.

claims.claim: In the final submodule of both 3P4S and 4P3S experiments, the current flowing through the TR cell was approximately zero, caused by rapid TR inside the cell due to overall heating from preceding TR cells leaving insufficient time for electricity transfer.
claims.quote: In the final submodule of both experiments, the current was approximately 0 in Figs. 16 (a) and (b)... The vanished current in the last submodule may have been caused by the rapid TR inside the cell due to the overall heating by the TR cells before.

claims.claim: The maximum amount of electricity transferred from normal cells to the TR cell in a parallel-connected submodule was 1.076 Ah, occurring in Submodule 2 of the 3P4S experiment.
claims.quote: Based on the calculation, the maximum amount of transferred electricity was 1.076 Ah.

claims.claim: Heat conduction through the main face (contact surface between cells) was the principal heat-transfer pathway during TR propagation in the battery modules tested, with combustion events not significantly affecting thermal propagation behaviour in the open chamber.
claims.quote: The heat conduction through the main face was the principle path of heat-transfer during the TR propagation process... the combustion event did not significantly affect the thermal propagation behaviour of the battery modules with different connections in the open chamber.

claims.claim: Nearly 15% of new energy vehicle accidents were caused by mechanical abuse conditions.
claims.quote: Data show that nearly 15% of new energy vehicle accidents were caused by mechanical abuse conditions.

claims.claim: The GA-BP mechanical model achieves a correlation coefficient R2 greater than 0.999 between model predictions and experimental results for battery mechanical properties under multiple SOC conditions.
claims.quote: The testing sets show that the correlation coefficient R2 between the GA-BP mechanical model and experimental results is greater than 0.999, which is better than those of the cubic fitting model, spring-damping equivalent mechanical model.

claims.claim: The average safety margin of the mechanical soft short-circuit failure warning for the GA-BP mechanical model is greater than 29%, compared to 23% for the model in reference [33].
claims.quote: the average safety margin of the mechanical soft short-circuit for the GA-BP mechanical model is greater than 29%, which is better than the 23% of the model in ref. [33].

claims.claim: Battery strength and force increase as SOC increases because more lithium ions are embedded in the negative electrode at higher SOC.
claims.quote: the strength and the force of the battery increase as more lithium ions are embedded in the negative electrode with the rise of SOC.

claims.claim: During the first half of compression (less than 4 mm), the force growth trend is linear due to extrusion of the space between the shell casing and the jellyroll.
claims.quote: During the first-half-stage of the experiment (less than 4 mm), the punch compresses the space between the shell casing and the jellyroll, the force growth trend presents linear characteristics for the reason of the extrusion.

claims.claim: The mechanical soft short-circuit failure signal precedes the electrical soft short-circuit signal, which in turn precedes the thermal soft short-circuit signal, because the mechanical characteristics immediately reflect internal damage while electrical and thermal characteristics remain unchanged until short-circuit and electrochemical reactions progress.
claims.quote: The soft short-circuit of the electrical and thermal signals lag behind that of the mechanical signal and advance to the actual mechanical failure of the battery. The mechanical failure and electrical failure of the battery obviously advance to the thermal failure of the battery. This is because the mechanical characteristics immediately reflect the decline in the first derivative of the force of the displacement when the internal structure of the battery is compressed and slightly damaged.

claims.claim: The thermal soft short-circuit has the lowest warning weight among mechanical, electrical, and thermal signals because of the low radial thermal conductivity of the battery.
claims.quote: Due to the low radial thermal conductivity of the battery, the thermal soft short-circuit warning weight is the lowest of the three.

claims.claim: At SOC=0.2, the maximum temperatures under the rigid rod punch test and hemispherical punch test are 69.3°C and 119.1°C respectively, with no violent thermal runaway and the battery structure remaining basically intact.
claims.quote: when SOC=0.2, the maximum temperatures under the rigid rod punch test and hemispherical punch test are 69.3°C and 119.1°C, respectively. There is no violent thermal runaway, and the battery structure is basically intact.

claims.claim: At SOC=0.6, severe gas injection and thermal runaway occur under both rigid rod punch and hemispherical punch tests, with maximum battery temperature exceeding 300°C and severe structural damage.
claims.quote: when SOC=0.6, severe gas injection and thermal runaway occur, the maximum temperature of the battery exceeds 300°C, and the battery structure is severely damaged.

claims.claim: Reducing the SOC of batteries not in direct contact with the external load (rear batteries) increases the average failure displacement by 14.2% and 16.5% for SOC=0.5 and SOC=0.2 conditions respectively, compared to all batteries at SOC=1.
claims.quote: With the decrease in the SOCs of Battery 2 and Battery 3, the average increase in the failure displacement is 14.2% and 16.5% for Test 2 and Test 3 compared with that of Test 1, respectively.

claims.claim: It is generally impossible to change the SOCs of batteries that directly contact the external load during mechanical abuse because these batteries often fail first.
claims.quote: In practical applications, it is generally impossible to change the SOCs of batteries that directly contact the external load because these batteries often fail first.

claims.claim: Reducing module SOC from high to medium increases failure displacement by 12.3%, 13.9%, and 11.5% for 6-, 9-, and 20-battery modules respectively; further reducing to low SOC increases failure displacement by an additional 16.7%, 16.9%, and 13.4%.
claims.quote: By reducing the SOCs of the batteries in the module, the high SOC is reduced to the medium SOC, and the failure displacements of different modules increase by 12.3%, 13.9%, and 11.5%. Considering that there will still be violent thermal runaway in the medium SOC module, the module will be reduced to a low SOC state, and the failure displacement of different modules will further increase by 16.7%, 16.9%, and 13.4%.

claims.claim: In small batteries, when electrical soft short-circuit occurs, ohmic heat can be quickly transferred to the battery surface, causing simultaneous observable temperature rise and voltage drop, making electrical and thermal warning weights similar.
claims.quote: in some situations, the soft short-circuit warning weights of the electrical and thermal signals are similar, which is mainly because in small batteries, when the battery has an electrical soft short-circuit, the heat generated by the ohmic thermal effect can be quickly transferred to the surface of the battery, so the temperature rise and voltage drop can be observed at the same time.

claims.claim: In large capacity batteries, when battery components are destroyed during mechanical soft short-circuit, SOC does not decrease immediately, so battery voltage is maintained and the mechanical warning weight remains higher than electrical warning weight.
claims.quote: in a large capacity battery, when the battery component is destroyed, the SOC will not decrease immediately, so the battery voltage will be maintained. Due to the low radial thermal conductivity, the rise in the battery surface temperature will lag behind other warning signals.

claims.claim: The battery metal shell resistance is only 0.3%–0.6% of the crest force and can be ignored in simulation.
claims.quote: Wierzbicki and Sahraei discovered that the battery metal shell resistance is only 0.3%–0.6% of the crest force and thus can be ignored in the simulation.

claims.claim: Under high SOC module conditions, the strain is continuously transmitted and batteries in the module almost fail simultaneously, with the top layer batteries in direct contact with the external load showing the largest failure degree.
claims.quote: The strain of the battery is continuously transmitted at the high SOC module. The batteries in the module almost fail at the same time. The failure degree of the top layer batteries in direct contact with the external load is the largest.

claims.claim: Under low SOC module conditions after rapid SOC reduction of rear batteries, only high SOC batteries and batteries in limited space behind the external load fail, while other batteries remain in a relatively stable state with maximum temperature not exceeding 150°C.
claims.quote: by rapidly reducing the SOCs of the batteries behind the compression, the strain in the module is no longer continuous; only the high SOC batteries and the batteries in the limited space behind the external load fail, and the other batteries are still in a relatively stable state. Combined with the conclusions from Section 4.2, it can be seen that under the low SOC state, it is difficult to observe violent thermal runaway, and the maximum temperature is not more than 150°C.

claims.claim: Internal short circuit is the most common feature for all abuse conditions (mechanical, electrical, and thermal) of lithium ion batteries.
claims.quote: Internal short circuit is the most common feature for all the abuse conditions.

claims.claim: The thermal runaway mechanism follows a chain reaction pathway in which decomposition reactions of battery component materials occur sequentially as temperature rises.
claims.quote: The thermal runaway follows a mechanism of chain reactions, during which the decomposition reaction of the battery component materials occurs one after another.

claims.claim: Materials with higher energy density may have lower thermal stability, leading to safety problems such as thermal runaway.
claims.quote: the materials with higher energy density may have lower thermal stability, leading to safety problems, e.g., thermal runaway (TR).

claims.claim: The thermal stability order of cathode materials is LFP > LMO > NCM111 > NCA > LCO, with LFP as the most stable during thermal runaway.
claims.quote: The order of the thermal stability should be LFP>LMO>NCM111>NCA>LCO, with the LFP as the most stable cathode material during TR process.

claims.claim: The initial decomposition of SEI occurs at 80–120°C with a peak at approximately 100°C and may start as low as 57°C, but exothermic heat generation only becomes detectable at 80°C or higher in calorimetry.
claims.quote: The initial decomposition of SEI occurs at 80~120oC, with a peak locates at approximately 100oC. The onset temperature can be lower than 80oC, as Wang et al. reported that the SEI decomposition might start from a temperature as low as 57oC. However, the exothermic heat generation can only become detectable at 80oC or higher in the calorimetry.

claims.claim: The melting points of PE and PP separators are approximately 130°C and 170°C respectively, and separator melting is an endothermic process that causes a shutdown effect by closing pores and increasing cell resistance.
claims.quote: The melting points of the PE and PP separators are approximately 130oC and 170oC, respectively. The separator melting is an endothermic process... the separator displays a shutdown effect with a sharp increase in the cell resistance.

claims.claim: The collapse temperature of a separator with ceramic coating can be as high as 200–260°C, compared to approximately 130°C for PE and 170°C for PP/PP/PE/PP separators without ceramic coating.
claims.quote: The collapse temperature of the separator with ceramic coating can be as high as 200-260oC.

claims.claim: For a lithium ion battery cell with 180 Wh·kg⁻¹ specific energy at 100% SOC, the total electric energy available for release during internal short circuit (ΔH) equals 648 J·g⁻¹.
claims.quote: For a battery cell with 180Wh·kg-1, the ΔH equals 180×3600/1000=648 J·g-1.

claims.claim: During overdischarge, copper dissolution from the current collector occurs once the anode potential reaches 3.5 V; dissolved copper ions can migrate through the separator and form copper dendrites that cause internal short circuit.
claims.quote: The copper can be electrolyzed from the current collector into the electrolyte as Cu2+ ions, once the anode potential reaches the dissolution potential (3.5V) of the copper. Guo et al. found that the dissolved copper ions migrated through the membrane and formed copper dendrites on the cathode side with lower potential. With an unsuppressed growth, the copper dendrite could penetrate the membrane, resulting in severe ISC.

claims.claim: Among the four ISC scenarios modeled by Santhanagopalan et al., ISC between the aluminum current collector and the active material at the anode (scenario 3) is the most dangerous.
claims.quote: The results show that the scenario 3) may be the most dangerous among the four.

claims.claim: For NCM cathodes, the decomposition enthalpy increases with increasing Ni content: from 512.5 J·g⁻¹ for NCM111 (x=1/3) to 971.5 J·g⁻¹ for Ni₀.₈₅ composition.
claims.quote: The enthalpies (ΔH) of the exothermic reactions are listed in Table 4. The cathode with larger proportion of Ni in the NCM releases more heat during decomposition.

claims.claim: The onset temperature for LFP cathode decomposition is as high as 310°C, significantly higher than that of NCA (~160–170°C) and LCO (~200°C).
claims.quote: The onset temperature for the LFP decomposition was as high as 310oC.

claims.claim: During penetration of a lithium ion battery, approximately 75% of the total current passes through the external short circuit route (through the electrodes) rather than through the nail itself.
claims.quote: The simulation results indicated that the current passing through the route No. 2 occupied approximately 75% of the total current during penetration.

claims.claim: During nail penetration, as much as 70% of the cell energy is released intensively within 60 seconds, and penetration at the edge of the electrode where heat dissipation is insufficient is more dangerous.
claims.quote: The results indicated that as much as 70% energy released intensively within 60s, leading to notable temperature rise. The thermal hazard during penetration can be influenced by the location of the nail. The penetration at the edge of the electrode where the heat dissipation is insufficient will be more dangerous.

claims.claim: A slight overcharge to SOC lower than 120% once causes no obvious capacity degradation in NCM+LMO composite cathode cells, but overcharge to 130% or higher SOC once causes considerable capacity loss.
claims.quote: Ouyang et al. found that no obvious capacity degradation occurs if the cell with NCM+LMO composite cathode was overcharged to an state of charge (SOC) lower than 120% once, but considerable capacity loss was observed when the cell was overcharged to 130% or higher SOC once.

claims.claim: The measured internal temperature of large format lithium ion batteries during thermal runaway is similar for cases with and without external combustion, indicating that combustion energy heats the surroundings rather than the cell interior.
claims.quote: the measured internal temperature of the cell was similar for the TR cases either with or without combustion as reported in [75], indicating that the combustion energy is not fully utilized to heat the cell, but heat the surroundings.

claims.claim: The spontaneous (self-induced) internal short circuit originates from contamination or defects during manufacturing and can take several days or even months to develop into ISC with obvious heat generation.
claims.quote: The spontaneous ISC is believed to originate from contamination or defects during manufacturing. It takes several days or even months for the contamination/defects to develop into spontaneous ISC with obvious heat generation.

claims.claim: The correlation between the total heat generation from internal short circuit (ΔH) and cell state of charge is not linear and requires further investigation.
claims.quote: The correlation between the ΔH and the cell SOC is not linear, and requires further investigation.

claims.claim: Under argon atmosphere, the peak concentration of carbon monoxide during thermal runaway was reduced by nearly 50% compared to air atmosphere, for a Samsung 18650 battery at 100% SOC, 200 W heating power, and 1.5 L/s intake speed.
claims.quote: Without the support of oxygen in the air, the content of carbon monoxide was significantly reduced. The peak concentrations of carbon monoxide changed by nearly 50%.

claims.claim: The concentration of CO2 during thermal runaway in air atmosphere (1989 ppm) was approximately 50% higher than in argon atmosphere (961 ppm) under otherwise identical conditions.
claims.quote: The concentration of carbon dioxide in the air environment is 1989 ppm, while in the argon environment it is 961 ppm, a drop close to 50%.

claims.claim: Under different SOCs, the concentrations of CO and CO2 produced during thermal runaway decreased as the SOC of the lithium-ion battery decreased.
claims.quote: Under different SOCs, the concentrations of CO and CO2 decreased as the charge capacity of the lithium-ion batteries decreased.

claims.claim: At an ambient temperature of 180 °C, the concentration of CO produced during thermal runaway was higher than at 220 °C, attributed to the ambient temperature being close to the valve opening temperature where electrolyte and membrane fully react.
claims.quote: The carbon monoxide concentration curves at 180 °C is different from other curves. The reason for this turn is that the ambient temperature is close to that of lithium-ion batteries valve opening temperature. The temperature of the valve at which the electrolyte and the membrane inside lithium-ion batteries are fully reacted for fusion, and the concentration of carbon monoxide produced is also high.

claims.claim: The concentrations of CO and CO2 during thermal runaway decreased with decreasing electric heating power applied to the lithium-ion battery.
claims.quote: Under different electric heating power, the concentrations of CO and CO2 decreased with the decrease of the electric heating power.

claims.claim: At a gas flow rate of 2.0 L/s argon, the battery temperature decreased significantly due to the large airflow, causing measurement result deviation; 1.5 L/s was selected as the appropriate flow rate for accurate results.
claims.quote: when the gas flow rate was 2.0 L/s, the temperature decreased much more due to the large airflow, which was affected by the argon gas flow. This phenomenon indicates that the higher flow rate results the measurement results deviation. According to the measurement results, it is reasonable to select the argon gas flow rate 1.5 L/s as the measurement speed to achieve accurate results.

claims.claim: The synthesis uncertainty of CO concentration in the initial stage of thermal runaway of lithium-ion batteries was much greater than that during the open stage of the outlet valve, enabling prediction of thermal runaway risk from CO concentration uncertainty changes.
claims.quote: the synthesis uncertainty of carbon monoxide concentration in the initial stage of thermal runaway of lithium-ion batteries was much greater than that of the data when the ventilation valve was open. The safety uncertainty caused by CO concentration can be determined with this method. When the uncertainty reaches a certain level, possible thermal runaway can be predicted.

claims.claim: The circumferential strain-rate first exceeds 0.1 με/s at 302 s, while venting occurs at 413 s and thermal runaway at 443 s, providing a lead time of over 100 s compared to conventional venting-based alerts and more than 140 s ahead of temperature-based confirmation.
claims.quote: It shows that the circumferential strain-rate first exceeds 0.1 με/s at 302 s, while venting occurs at 413 s and thermal runaway at 443 s. This corresponds to a lead time of over 100s compared to conventional venting-based alerts, and more than 140 s ahead of temperature-based confirmation.

claims.claim: The strain-rate threshold of 0.2 με/s is reached at 345 s, corresponding to activation of the An-ELE reaction and providing a practical early warning signal during rapid gas accumulation.
claims.quote: The strain-rate threshold of 0.2 με/s is reached at 345 s, providing a practical early warning signal during rapid gas accumulation.

claims.claim: Under fully constrained boundary conditions, maximum strain levels are significantly lower with greater delay compared to two-sided clamping, because full structural confinement restricts radial expansion and causes pressure-induced volumetric changes to be accommodated as compressive stress accumulation rather than external deformation.
claims.quote: under the fully constrained condition shown in Fig. 7(c) and (d), the maximum strain levels are significantly lower, and the strain growth occurs with greater delay... full structural confinement markedly restricts radial expansion of the casing, causing the pressure-induced volumetric changes to be accommodated internally as compressive stress accumulation rather than external deformation.

claims.claim: The biaxial clamping configuration triggers the earliest warning response, with the initial strain-rate increase observed at tst = 283 s and the onset of critical expansion at tac = 327 s, whereas the free-expansion and fully constrained cases exhibit tst = 302 s and tst = 345 s respectively.
claims.quote: the biaxial clamping configuration triggers the earliest warning response, with the initial strain-rate increase observed at tst = 283 s and the onset of critical expansion at tac = 327s... both the free-expansion and fully constrained cases exhibit similar response timings, with tst = 302 and tac = 310 s, and tst = 345 s and tac = 337 s, respectively

claims.claim: Circumferential strain consistently exhibits the highest amplitude and strain rate throughout thermal runaway progression, while axial strain response is the weakest with minimal changes in both strain and strain rate.
claims.quote: the εphi consistently exhibits the highest amplitude and strain rate throughout the process, demonstrating strong sensitivity to structural swelling... the axial strain response is the weakest, with minimal changes in both strain and strain rate, suggesting a delayed and less sensitive reaction to pressure-driven deformation.

claims.claim: Near the geometric centre of the cylindrical cell, the strain magnitude reaches a peak of 13,000 με, but rapidly attenuates as the measurement point moves radially outward, stabilizing beyond r > 4 mm, where values drop below 2000 με under fully constrained conditions.
claims.quote: Near the geometric centre of the cylindrical cell, the strain magnitude reaches its peak to 13,000 με. However, as the measurement point moves radially outward, the strain rapidly attenuates and tends to stabilize beyond r > 4 mm, especially under fully constrained conditions, where values drop below 2000 με.

claims.claim: The model adopts a one-way coupling strategy with no feedback from the structural domain to the thermal or chemical subsystems, and temperature-induced strain is neglected because thermal expansion dominates deformation in prismatic LIBs while electrochemically induced strain is more significant in cylindrical cells.
claims.quote: the present model adopts a one-way coupling strategy: temperature evolution drives the reaction rate and gas generation, pressure development depends solely on gas accumulation, and structural response is driven by the pressure fields. There is no feedback from the structural domain to the thermal or chemical subsystems. Moreover, thermal expansion is known to dominate deformation behaviour in prismatic LIBs, while electrochemically induced strain is more significant in cylindrical cells.

claims.claim: Surface temperature signals often lag during early stages of internal heating due to thermal conduction delay, limiting the responsiveness of thermal-based early warning approaches.
claims.quote: due to the thermal conduction delay during the early stages of internal heating, surface temperature signals often lag, thereby limiting the responsiveness of thermal-based early warning approaches.

claims.claim: The safety valve of the Samsung ICR18650-26H M type 18650 LIB opened when the battery surface temperature reached 132°C–167°C, releasing pre-runaway phase gas (white smoke).
claims.quote: The safety valve opened when the battery was heated to a surface temperature of 132°C–167°C (Ping et al., 2018), then it released the pre-runaway phase gas (white smoke).

claims.claim: Dramatic thermal runaway and deflagration occurred in the 18650 LIB when its surface temperature rose to 199°C–225°C.
claims.quote: With the heating of the battery, there was dramatic runaway and deflagration as its temperature rose to 199°C–225°C (Ping et al., 2018), with the gas venting radially from the air leaking holes (six as a circle) on top of the battery.

claims.claim: The LEL of vented gas from LIB thermal runaway increases initially and then declines as SOC increases, with the highest LEL occurring at 60% SOC.
claims.quote: the LEL of the vented gas increases in initial stage and then declines with the increase of the batteries SOCs... the highest LEL is at 60% SOC.

claims.claim: At 100% SOC, the LIB vented gas has the lowest LEL among the SOC values tested, representing the highest explosion risk during thermal runaway.
claims.quote: It is better not to keep the SOC as 100%, which has a lowest LEL and a high risk of danger when a thermal runaway happens.

claims.claim: The LEL of vented gas from LIB thermal runaway declines with increasing heating temperature, because higher temperature produces more violent runaway with a higher proportion of flammable gas.
claims.quote: the LEL of the vented gas declines with the increase of heating temperature in general. The higher temperature when the thermal runaway happens makes the runaway more violent, and the proportion of flammable gas is higher.

claims.claim: The LEL of vented gas from LIB thermal runaway declines with increasing heating power, with the lowest LEL recorded being 4.80% in the study.
claims.quote: the LEL of the vented gas declines with the increase of heating power obviously... The lowest LEL is 4.80% in this work.

claims.claim: At low SOC, adequate reaction between battery electrolyte and air during thermal runaway produces large amounts of CO2 (a non-flammable gas), which raises the LEL of the vented gas mixture.
claims.quote: the LEL increases when the SOC is at a low level, which is the result of less violent thermal runaway and battery electrolyte can react with the air adequately. This combustion reaction produces a large amount of carbon dioxide (CO2), which makes the LEL higher as a kind of non-flammable gas.

claims.claim: At high SOC, internal battery materials cannot react with air adequately during thermal runaway, producing more CO (a flammable gas), which lowers the LEL of the vented gas.
claims.quote: The thermal runaway takes place dramatically when it comes to a high SOC stage, while the internal materials in the battery cannot react with the air timely and adequately, producing much CO. The CO makes the LEL lower as a kind of flammable gas.

claims.claim: The LEL values calculated using Le Chatelier Formula combined with the empirical formula for inert gas correction differed from instrument-tested LEL values by errors of 2.1%, 1.9%, and 0.4% for three samples, all less than 5%.
claims.quote: The errors of the two results of three samples are 2.1%, 1.9%, and 0.4%... The errors are all less than 5%.

claims.claim: Once the internal temperature of a LIB reaches 120°C, irreversible chemical changes occur inside the battery, greatly increasing the risk of runaway and explosion.
claims.quote: Once the temperature goes to 120°C, the inside of the battery will undergo an irreversible chemical change and the risk of runaway and explosion increases greatly.

claims.claim: The carbon monoxide content in LIB thermal runaway vented gas ranges between 2.0% and 9.9%, and CO is considered the major lethal toxic agent in fires, capable of causing asphyxia, narcosis, tissue hypoxia, loss of consciousness, organ failure, and death.
claims.quote: The content of carbon monoxide in vented gas is between 2.0% and 9.9% and there is still a belief that it is the major lethal toxic agent in fires, which can lead to asphyxia, narcosis tissue hypoxia, loss of consciousness, organ failure and death.

claims.claim: The LIB electrolyte becomes unstable when its temperature reaches 80–100°C.
claims.quote: Electrolyte, as a kind of organic solvents, will be unstable when its temperature reaches 80–100°C.

claims.claim: The self-heating temperature (T1) of a fresh 18650 LIB cell is 95.8 °C, and it decreases to 64.6, 54.5, 47.4, and 41 °C after overcharge to 105%, 110%, 115%, and 120% SOC, respectively, representing a decrease of 54.8 °C at 120% SOC compared to the fresh cell.
claims.quote: T1 of the fresh cell that has not suffered from overcharge is 95.8 °C, while T1 of the cells that are overcharged to 105% SOC, 110% SOC, 115% SOC, and 120% SOC are 64.6, 54.5, 47.4, and 41 °C, respectively. Compared with that of the fresh cell, T1 of the cell overcharged to 120% SOC decreased by 54.8 °C, which means that even within the normal temperature range of the thermal management system, thermal runaway may be triggered.

claims.claim: During slight overcharge, conductivity loss (CL) and loss of lithium inventory (LLI) always occur, while loss of active material (LAM) begins only when the cell is overcharged beyond a certain degree (between 105% and 110% SOC).
claims.quote: During the slight overcharge process, CL and LLI always happen, while LAM occurs only when the cell is overcharged to a certain degree (105−110% SOC). Lithium plating is the main reason for LLI; the amount of plated lithium increases with the increase of the overcharge degree.

claims.claim: The arrival time of the dV/dt peak during relaxation voltage analysis decreases linearly with increasing overcharge degree, indicating that the content of reversible lithium decreases linearly with increasing overcharge degree.
claims.quote: It is very interesting to find that the arrival time of the dV/dt peak decreases linearly with the increase of the overcharge degree, which suggests that the content of reversible lithium decreases linearly with the increase of the overcharge degree.

claims.claim: The thermal runaway triggering temperature (T2) is not significantly affected by overcharge degree: T2 for the fresh cell is 163 °C and for the cell overcharged to 120% SOC is 161.2 °C, showing almost no difference.
claims.quote: overcharge has little impact on T2. T2 for the fresh cell is 163 °C, and that for the cell overcharged to 120% SOC is 161.2 °C. There is almost no difference between them. T2 generally represents internal short circuit, which means that the separator collapses. The separator is a polymer, and the side reactions inside the cell have little effect on its melting point.

claims.claim: As overcharge degree increases from 105% to 120% SOC, the interval time (Δt) between sudden voltage drop and thermal runaway triggering increases by approximately 3.5 times (from 2656.6 s to 9215.1 s), while the temperature at sudden voltage drop (TV) decreases by 31 °C.
claims.quote: As the overcharge degree increases, the interval time (Δt) increases significantly. For the cell overcharged to 105% SOC, the interval time (Δt) is 2656.6 s. It is 9215.1 s for the cell overcharged to 120% SOC. The interval time (Δt) increases by around 3.5 times. As for the temperature (TV), it decreases obviously with the increase of the overcharge degree. The temperature (TV) for the cell overcharged to 120% SOC is 79.1 °C, which is 31 °C lower than that of the cell overcharged to 105% SOC.

claims.claim: The decrease in self-heating temperature T1 caused by overcharge is primarily attributed to lithium plating and the high reactivity of plated metallic lithium, which can react with the electrolyte at lower temperatures than lithium intercalated into graphite.
claims.quote: The decrease of T1 is mainly due to lithium plating and the decomposition of the SEI film at the anode... The plated lithium has high reactivity, which can participate in side reactions at lower temperatures. Thus, overcharge lowers T1.

claims.claim: Gas generated inside the cell during overcharge is the primary reason for the increase of interval time (Δt) and the decrease of voltage-drop temperature (TV), because accumulated gas advances CID activation by building internal pressure.
claims.quote: gas is generated by side reactions during the overcharge process, which is the major reason for the increase of the interval time (Δt) and the decrease of the temperature (TV).

claims.claim: Internal gas composition of a cell overcharged to 120% SOC contains CO2 (62.23%), N2 (16.45%), CO, CH4, and C2H6, with CO2 accounting for more than half of all gas species generated by side reactions.
claims.quote: Except for N2, four gaseous species are generated by a slight overcharge, namely, carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), methane (CH4), and ethane (C2H6). Among them, CO2 accounts for 62.23%, more than half.

claims.claim: XRD analysis of the anode overcharged to 120% SOC shows no new diffraction peaks and no peak shifts, indicating that slight overcharge has no effect on the anode crystal structure.
claims.quote: Comparing the XRD diffraction peaks of the fresh anode and the overcharged anode, it can be found that no new diffraction peaks appear and no diffraction peaks are shifted. This indicates that a slight overcharge has no effect on the anode structure of the cell.

claims.claim: When a cell is overcharged to 167% SOC, the cell ruptures and all stored energy is released suddenly.
claims.quote: When overcharged to 167% SOC, the cell ruptures and all the stored energy is released suddenly.

claims.claim: SEI decomposition begins at 80–120°C and is considered the start of battery self-heating in lithium-ion batteries.
claims.quote: The decomposition of solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) at 80–120 C is considered as the beginning of battery self-heating.

claims.claim: Separator failure occurs when temperature reaches the melting point of the separator, resulting in large-scale internal short circuit (ISC).
claims.quote: The separator fails when the temperature reaches the melting point of the separator, resulting in large-scale internal short circuit (ISC). A large amount of heat and gas will be released, leading to TR or combustion and explosion of batteries.

claims.claim: In the simulation at 107 kW·m⁻³ heating power, ISC Joule heat accounted for 26.3% and cathode decomposition accounted for 11.1% of the self-reaction heat, making ISC the main factor leading to battery TR among internal reactions.
claims.quote: the heat generated by ISC and cathode decomposition accounted for 26.3% and 11.1%, respectively in the self-reaction heat of the battery... Therefore, ISC was the main factor leading to battery TR in the self-reaction heat of the battery according to the simulation result.

claims.claim: At a heating power of 143 kW·m⁻³, thermal runaway cannot occur when the air convection coefficient is higher than 15 W·m⁻²·K⁻¹.
claims.quote: The TR cannot occur at 143 kW∙m(cid:0)3 when the air convection coefficient was higher than 15 W⋅m(cid:0)2⋅K(cid:0)1.

claims.claim: With increasing heating power per unit volume from 95 kW·m⁻³ to 155 kW·m⁻³, the TR critical time decreased and TR critical temperature increased.
claims.quote: With the increase of heating power per unit volume from 95 kW∙m(cid:0)3 to 155 kW∙m(cid:0)3, the TR critical time decreased and TR critical temperature increased.

claims.claim: When no thermal insulation exists between the clamp and battery, the heat conduction of the clamp is the main heat transfer path of the battery pack, meaning clamp thermal resistance must be considered in TR propagation models.
claims.quote: It can be inferred that the heat conduction of the clamp was the main heat transfer path of the battery pack when there was no heat insulation layer between the clamp and the battery. Therefore, the influence of clamp on battery pack should be considered when discussing the TR propagation model of battery pack.

claims.claim: Increasing the battery gap reduces the TR critical time of the first battery (Bat1) because more heat is concentrated on Bat1, but increases the TR propagation delay period between batteries due to reduced inter-battery heat transfer.
claims.quote: More heat was concentrated on Bat1 with increasing gap, thus shortening the critical time of Bat1 TR. Since the heat transfer was significantly reduced with the increase of the gap, the TR propagation delay period of the battery pack increased.

claims.claim: ISC is modeled as occurring when the internal battery temperature reaches 160°C, at which point stored electrical energy of 1.50×10⁵ J is released over approximately 35 seconds.
claims.quote: When the internal temperature reaches 160 C, the ISC occurs and the electric energy is released. The duration of battery TR is about 35 s according to the experimental results. Therefore, the release time (Δt) of electric energy is set to 35 s here.
